text: I want the beam dead center with no tilt at all.
Put the beam in the center, untilted.
Shift the beam slightly to the left.
Move the laser a bit to the right.
Push it far to the left.
Push it way over to the right side.
Place the beam at the extreme left edge of the range.
Set the beam about one-third of the way from center to the right.
Just barely nudge the beam to the right of center.
Put it moderately to the left, about halfway between center and the edge.
Apply a strong negative tilt, nothing else.
Add a small positive angle to the beam.
Tilt the beam as far negative as it will go.
Give it the maximum positive tilt.
A very mild positive tilt, almost negligible.
Moderate negative tilt, beam stays centered.
Tilt it about halfway in the positive direction.
Just a tiny negative angle, almost zero.
Raise the beam upward significantly.
Drop the beam down a little bit.
Put the beam near the top of the vertical range.
Move the beam to the very bottom vertically.
Shift the beam slightly upward from center.
Nudge it just a hair below the vertical center.
Position the beam about three-quarters up from the bottom.
Place it at moderate height, noticeably above center.
Shift the laser to the right and tilt it negatively.
Push it far left with strong positive tilt.
Beam slightly right, slightly up, with mild positive tilt.
Move the laser to the upper left corner with no tilt.
Lower-right position, moderate negative tilt.
Center horizontally, shift up a bit, add a small positive angle.
Upper right with strong positive tilt.
Just barely left, barely down, minimal negative tilt.
Max out everything to the left and bottom with extreme negative tilt.
Everything to the maximum: far right, far up, extreme positive tilt.
Crank the position to the right as far as possible, keep tilt zero.
Put the beam at the absolute bottom, all the way to the left.
Maximum positive tilt with no positional offset.
Upper-left corner, maximum negative tilt.
Place the beam right at center, no offset, no tilt, nothing.
Almost centered but with just the tiniest rightward shift.
The beam should be basically centered, maybe a hair above and slightly tilted positive.
Keep it very close to center, shift one tiny step to the left and one tiny step down.
I want it near the origin with a barely-there negative tilt.
Basically neutral — just the faintest positive x-offset and the faintest negative y-offset.
Can you just, like, move the spot a smidge to the left? And maybe tilt it a little?
Yeet the beam all the way to the right and angle it hard.
Scoot it down a bit and tilt it clockwise.
Hmm, something in the middle-ish but kind of upper-right if you know what I mean.
Make it look like it's leaning left.
Put the laser beam roughly 70% to the right and halfway up.
I want a kinda off-center beam, maybe like 60-40 to the right? No angle.
Give me a nice diagonal setup — right and up, with matching tilt.
Shift the beam to the right without any tilt.
Make the beam offset.
A beam with some angle on it.
Tilt the beam. Not too much, not too little.
Move it up and to the left somehow.
I want the beam somewhere between center and the far right.
Give me a strong setup, everything maxed in the positive direction.
Like the previous one but the opposite direction.
